skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar, talking-head, and lip-sync videos on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes across ByteDance OmniHuman, Wan-AI Wan 2-7, HappyHorse 1.0, and Seedance v2 Pro. Picks the right model for the user's intent and ships each model's documented prompting patterns plus the minimal `runcomfy run` invoke.
skill.summary: Route user requests to the right audio-driven avatar model on RunComfy and generate talking-head videos via the `runcomfy` CLI.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 67 'external_commands' and 36 'network' locations, but all are documentation examples of `runcomfy run` CLI invocations in fenced code blocks and reference URLs to runcomfy.com. The skill's `allowed-tools` is explicitly restricted to `Bash(runcomfy *)`, and the skill never instructs the agent to run arbitrary shell commands. The filesystem finding at line 286 is standard CLI token storage at `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json`. The skill includes a thorough security section covering consent, token storage, shell-injection boundaries, and indirect prompt injection mitigations.

low_findings.title: CLI Tool Invocation via Bash
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run `runcomfy` CLI commands to generate avatar videos. While the `allowed-tools` declaration restricts execution to `Bash(runcomfy *)`, the agent will invoke external commands on the user's system. This is by design but warrants user awareness. All 67 code-block references to shell commands are documentation examples for the `runcomfy run` CLI — no arbitrary shell execution is instructed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static findings are all `runcomfy run` CLI examples in fenced code blocks. The `allowed-tools` frontmatter restricts Bash to `runcomfy *` only. No arbitrary shell commands are instructed.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs to External Services
low_findings.description: The skill contains numerous URLs to runcomfy.com model catalog pages and example placeholder URLs (your-cdn.example). These are documentation references and example inputs, not actual network requests made by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network findings are documentation links to runcomfy.com and placeholder URLs in code examples. No data exfiltration or hidden network behavior.

low_findings.title: Token Storage in Home Directory
low_findings.description: The skill documents that `runcomfy login` stores the API token at `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` with mode 0600. This is standard CLI credential storage practice, not a security concern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token storage path is documented in the security section as standard practice with file mode 0600. This is expected CLI behavior.

content.user_title: Generate AI Avatar and Talking-Head Videos
content.value_statement: Users need to create lip-synced avatar videos but don't know which AI model to use. This skill routes requests to the right RunComfy model — OmniHuman, Wan 2-7, HappyHorse, or Seedance v2 — and generates the correct command in one step.
actual_capabilities: Classifies user intent to pick the correct audio-driven avatar model from five options
Generates `runcomfy run` commands with the correct JSON input for OmniHuman, Wan 2-7, HappyHorse 1.0, Seedance v2 Pro, and Wan 2-2 Animate
Provides documented prompting patterns for each model including scene description, audio tone, and camera language
Handles five common use-case patterns: UGC ads, multi-language brand videos, stylized mascots, script-to-video, and cinematic monologues
Routes between t2v and i2v endpoints based on whether the user has an existing portrait or a text script
limitations: Requires the user to install and authenticate the `runcomfy` CLI separately
Audio-driven avatar models require consent for both likeness and voice cloning — the skill cannot verify rights
Does not generate the source portrait image — users need to provide one or use a companion image-generation skill
HappyHorse 1.0 regenerates audio each call, so lip-sync to a specific MP3 requires Wan 2-7 or OmniHuman instead

use_cases.title: Create a UGC product ad with a voiceover
use_cases.description: A marketer has a portrait photo and a voiceover MP3. The skill picks OmniHuman and generates a single `runcomfy run` command to produce a vertical talking-head video.

use_cases.title: Generate a talking-head video from a written script
use_cases.description: A content creator has only a script, no audio file. The skill picks HappyHorse 1.0 t2v and crafts a prompt that quotes the spoken line for in-pass audio generation.

use_cases.title: Dub a brand video into multiple languages
use_cases.description: A brand team needs the same presenter speaking different languages. The skill uses OmniHuman with the same portrait and different audio files per language, producing dubbed clips with consistent identity.

prompt_templates.title: Basic avatar from portrait and audio
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a portrait image at [URL] and a voiceover MP3 at [URL]. Use OmniHuman to generate a talking-head video. Output to ./out.

prompt_templates.title: Talking head from script only (no audio file)
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a script: "Welcome to our product demo. Today we'll show you three things." Generate a vertical 9:16 talking-head video with HappyHorse 1.0 t2v. Keep the clip under 6 seconds.

prompt_templates.title: Stylized character with audio
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an illustrated mascot PNG at [URL] and a voiceover at [URL]. Use Wan 2-2 Animate to animate the character speaking the audio. Output to ./out.

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic monologue with full scene control
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cinematic monologue using Seedance v2 Pro. Reference subject at [URL], reference audio at [URL]. Prompt: "Anamorphic close-up, golden hour light through window, shallow DoF." Duration 10s, aspect 21:9.

output_examples.input: I have a portrait at https://cdn.example/presenter.jpg and a voiceover at https://cdn.example/voice.mp3. Make a vertical talking-head video.
Write me a 5-second video of a woman saying "Hi, I am Aria." No audio file available.
output: Command: runcomfy run happyhorse/happyhorse-1-0/text-to-video --input '{"prompt": "A woman in her 30s looks at the camera and says clearly: \"Hi, I am Aria.\" Audio: friendly tone.", "duration": 5}' --output-dir ./out

best_practices: Always check whether the user has an audio file or just a script before picking a model — this determines t2v vs audio_url routing
Use OmniHuman as the default for portrait + audio jobs; only escalate to Seedance v2 Pro when cinematic composition is required
Quote spoken lines exactly with `says clearly: "..."` in HappyHorse prompts, otherwise the model paraphrases or skips speech
anti_patterns: Do not pick Seedance v2 Pro for simple portrait-talks jobs — it is overpowered and slower than OmniHuman for that use case
Do not put spoken words in Wan 2-7 prompts — the model syncs to the audio waveform, not prompt text
Do not instruct the agent to run arbitrary shell commands — the `allowed-tools` scope is `Bash(runcomfy *)` only

faq.question: Which model should I use for a simple portrait-talks job?
faq.answer: Use OmniHuman. It takes one portrait image and one audio file and produces a natural talking-head video. It is the default route and the simplest path.

faq.question: What if the user has no audio file?
faq.answer: Use HappyHorse 1.0 text-to-video. It generates speech in-pass from the prompt. Quote the spoken line exactly inside the prompt for best results.

faq.question: Can I use this for stylized or illustrated characters?
faq.answer: Yes. Use Wan 2-2 Animate for anime, illustration, or mascot characters. For photoreal portraits, use OmniHuman or Wan 2-7 instead.

faq.question: How do I get precise lip-sync to a specific MP3?
faq.answer: Use Wan 2-7 with the `audio_url` field. The audio file drives the mouth motion directly, giving you locked lip-sync to your specific track.

faq.question: What are the consent requirements for avatar generation?
faq.answer: You must have rights to both the subject's likeness and the speaker's voice. The skill cannot verify consent — the user is responsible. Do not target real people without permission.

faq.question: What does the skill need installed?
faq.answer: The `runcomfy` CLI. Install with `npm i -g @runcomfy/cli` and authenticate with `runcomfy login` or by setting the `RUNCOMFY_TOKEN` environment variable.
